page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-288-metal is a bare-metal server instance featuring 288 vCPUs mapped to 144 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor. Operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz, this x86_64 architecture platform includes a 504 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and 8-bit integer operations. The system is equipped with 1080 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing 3.75 GB of RAM per core. This configuration does not include local storage or onboard GPU hardware.

Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 313,180.49 and a PassMark CPU score of 178,785.41. Memory performance is exceptionally strong, achieving read-only cached bandwidth of 23,040.71 GB/sec and uncached write bandwidth of 428.11 GB/sec. The platform excels in cryptographic operations, database transactions, and static web serving, where it recorded a throughput of 164.82 GB/sec. Single-threaded performance is also robust, though image processing and small-model LLM text generation benchmarks show relatively lower performance percentiles compared to its massive multi-threaded capabilities.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-standard-288-metal offers high resource density and exceptional compute-per-resource value for highly parallelized workloads, avoiding the premium associated with GPU-accelerated instances. The lack of bundled local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which should be factored into architectural planning. This instance is highly suited for compute-intensive tasks, large-scale database operations, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and medium-to-large LLM inference prompt processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-288-metal is a high-density bare-metal server powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with 288 vCPUs (144 physical cores) running at 4.2 GHz. It features 1080 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 6 GHz, delivering 3.75 GB of memory per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and outstanding database operations and static web serving throughput. While single-threaded tasks and small-model text generation are less optimal, the server provides excellent qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized workloads. It is best suited for large-scale database operations, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and medium-to-large LLM inference prompt processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-288-metal is a bare-metal server with 288 vCPUs, 1080 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-288-metal is a bare-metal server with 288 vCPUs and 1080 GB DDR5 RAM, optimized for high-compute workloads.
tagline: A high-density bare-metal compute platform featuring 288 Intel Xeon vCPUs and DDR5 memory for demanding parallel workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with 288 vCPUs and 144 physical cores running at 4.2 GHz.
1080 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz.
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database transaction performance.
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector and matrix math.
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware, requiring network-attached storage.
Optimized for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and parallel computing.
